The Zookeeper’s Wife (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,445,186Details
International Box Office $8,863,563
Worldwide Box Office $26,308,749
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,688,217
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,256,374
Total $3,944,591
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In 1939 Poland, Antonina Żabińska and her husband, Dr. Jan Żabiński have the Warsaw Zoo flourishing under his stewardship and her care. When their country is invaded by the Nazis, Jan and Antonina are stunned and forced to report to the Reich’s newly appointed chief zoologist, Lutz Heck. To fight back on their own terms, the Żabińskis covertly begin working with the Resistance and put into action plans to save lives out of what has become the Warsaw Ghetto, with Antonina putting herself and even her children at great risk.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$3,288,835 (18.9% of total gross)
Legs:5.30 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:66.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:541 opening theaters/1,057 max. theaters, 5.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $23,260,251

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 31st, 2017 (Limited) by Focus Features
International Releases: March 24th, 2017 (Wide) (Poland)
March 31st, 2017 (Wide), released as Úkryt v zoo (Czech Republic)
March 31st, 2017 (Wide), released as Umut Bahçesi (Turkey)
March 31st, 2017 (Wide) (Slovakia)
April 14th, 2017 (Wide) (Lithuania)
Video Release: July 4th, 2017 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic elements, disturbing images, violence, brief sexuality, nudity and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2464 (Cert #50083), 2/22/2017)
Running Time: 126 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Epilogue
Geographical setting: Poland
Plot point: Infidelity, Jewish, Nazis, Occupying Army, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance, War, Zoos
Social setting: Holocaust, World War II
Subgenre: War Drama
Time period setting: 1930s, 1940s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Focus Features, Scion Films, Electric City Entertainment, Tollin Productions, Rowe Miller Productions, LD Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In 1939 Poland, Antonina Żabińska and her husband, Dr. Jan Żabiński have the Warsaw Zoo flourishing under his stewardship and her care. When their country is invaded by the Nazis, Jan and Antonina are stunned and forced to report to the Reich’s newly appointed chief zoologist, Lutz Heck. To fight back on their own terms, the Żabińskis covertly begin working with the Resistance and put into action plans to save lives out of what has become the Warsaw Ghetto, with Antonina putting herself and even her children at great risk.
